title: eriogonum umbellatum
intro: Eriogonum umbellatum, also known as sulfur buckwheat or umbrella sage, is a perennial wildflower native to North America, characterized by its clusters of small, yellow or reddish flowers and a distinctive umbrella-shaped inflorescence. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, due to the presence of flavonoids and other compounds. Consuming Eriogonum umbellatum is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional or herbalist.
